name: replicate
description: Browse public models and run predictions on Replicate-hosted machine learning models

tools.name: List Models
tools.description: List public models available on Replicate

historicalSummary.omitted: version details, cover images, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Model
tools.description: Get details about a model, including its latest version ID

model_owner.description: Owner of the model, e.g. stability-ai

model_name.description: Name of the model, e.g. sdxl

tools.name: Create Prediction
tools.description: Run a model by creating a prediction from a version ID and input object

wait.description: Set to wait=60 to hold the request open up to 60 seconds for the prediction to finish

version.description: Model version ID (64-character hash from Get Model latest_version.id)

input.description: Model-specific input object, e.g. {"prompt":"a photo of an astronaut"}

tools.name: Get Prediction
tools.description: Get the status and output of a prediction

prediction_id.description: ID of the prediction to fetch
ID of the prediction to cancel

prompts.title: Find a model
prompts.prompt: Find a Replicate model for a task I describe and show me its latest version ID and inputs.

prompts.title: Run a prediction
prompts.prompt: Run a Replicate model with inputs I provide and report the output when it finishes.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Replicate API Setup

steps.title: Create a Replicate account
steps.description: Go to https://replicate.com and sign up (GitHub sign-in supported). New accounts get a small amount of free usage before billing is required.

steps.title: Create an API token
steps.description: Open https://replicate.com/account/api-tokens, give the token a descriptive name (e.g. 'Veryfront Integration'), and create it.

steps.title: Store the token
steps.description: Copy the token and add it to your .env file as REPLICATE_API_TOKEN=r8_...

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Models tool to confirm the token works. A 401 means the token is wrong or revoked.

notes: Predictions are billed per second of compute — costs vary widely by model and hardware
Create Prediction sends Prefer: wait=60 to return synchronously when possible; long-running models still return status 'starting' or 'processing' — poll with Get Prediction
Use the version ID from Get Model's latest_version.id field when creating predictions
